name: Operational Machine Scoring Guidance Uses Simulated Responses as Test-Suite Cases
statement: New Meridian's standard-process guidance provides direct operational support for one acceptable simulated-response use: machine-scoring test suites that validate scoring and scanning rules and procedures. This is a QA and implementation-check role, not evidence that synthetic responses can replace real student responses for automated scorer validation or live scoring decisions.
population_or_context: New Meridian operational test forms and state assessment programs using New Meridian item bank content for ELA/literacy and mathematics.
time_horizon: Full text retrieved and extracted on 2026-06-03.
quantitative_note: No response counts or statistical performance metrics are reported for the simulated test-suite cases.
caveats: The source concerns simulated test cases for rule-based machine scoring and scanning, not LLM-generated synthetic writing responses.
The source does not support synthetic-only automated scorer validation, fairness validation, cut scores, or live scoring decisions.
The process guidance does not specify how simulated responses are authored or reviewed.
evaluation_method: Review of simulated test cases to verify correct application of machine-scoring and scanning rules.
limitations: Bounded to machine-scored item QA in New Meridian operational form processes.
Does not address generative AI response augmentation or automated essay scoring model training.
Should be treated as an operational QA use category rather than validation evidence for a statistical or LLM scorer.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the definition of a test suite and the statement that New Meridian bank assets should include simulated responses to items.
